headlines.title: Chancellor to offer support over rising heating oil costs
headlines.description: Rachel Reeves says the Treasury is also looking at "different options" to help households most vulnerable to soaring energy bills.

headlines.title: Doomed hereditary peers spy chance to stay in the Lords
headlines.description: The Tories are deciding which hereditary peers to keep after being offered 15 seats in a compromise deal.

headlines.title: Rescuers blame weather and 'underprepared skiers' for rise in Alps avalanche deaths
headlines.description: The BBC joins a French Alps rescue team as the number of skiers killed this season passes 100.

headlines.title: 'We're not profiteering on fuel. But my staff still face abuse'
headlines.description: Independent retailer Goran Raven says the higher oil price is "horrific" for him as well as his customers.

headlines.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
headlines.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

headlines.title: Watch: Why Kharg Island is a lifeline for Iran
headlines.description: BBC Security Brief's Mikey Kay breaks down why Kharg Island is so strategically important for Tehran.

headlines.title: How the Iran war exposed cracks in Trump's Republican coalition
headlines.description: The president's most dedicated supporters are on his side - but significant numbers of Republicans have serious doubts.

headlines.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
headlines.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

headlines.title: Why has Trump eased sanctions on Russian oil - and will it help Putin?
headlines.description: The US said easing sanctions on Russian oil would provide only a limited financial boost to Putin.

headlines.title: Is Dubai's glossy image under threat? Not everyone thinks so
headlines.description: The city has been considered a safe haven by expats, tourists and influencers  - but that's now being tested.

headlines.title: 'Like a trap you can't escape': The women who regret being mothers
headlines.description: From mourning the life they no longer have to feeling never-ending pressure, women tell the BBC why they regret becoming mothers.

headlines.title: Why do Glasgow's historic buildings keep catching fire?
headlines.description: The fire that devastated a building on Union Street is the latest in a series of blazes to strike the city's historic sites.

headlines.title: They were told it wasn't for girls, but these could be the future faces of F1
headlines.description: F1 Academy wants to nurture and develop female drivers who can compete at motorsport’s top level

headlines.title: She spent 16 hours on Instagram. It's up to a jury to decide if Meta is to blame
headlines.description: A landmark lawsuit will set the stage for thousands of people who say social media platforms are intentionally addictive.

headlines.title: Explosion at Amsterdam Jewish school 'a deliberate attack' says mayor
headlines.description: Amsterdam mayor Femke Halsema condemned a "cowardly act of aggression" towards the Jewish community.

headlines.title: The Papers: Epstein files photo 'bombshell' and 'Iran war spirals'
headlines.description: The Middle East war and a photo Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor with Jeffrey Epstein and Peter Mandelson dominate Saturday's front pages.

headlines.title: 'Hate-watch classic' War of the Worlds sweeps Razzie Awards
headlines.description: Ice Cube's alien invasion adaptation receives five Razzies, which go to the year's worst movies.

headlines.title: Ethics adviser rejects Tory call for inquiry into PM over Mandelson appointment
headlines.description: Sir Laurie Magnus said the relevant process appeared to have been followed when Lord Mandelson was made ambassador.

headlines.title: Maya Jama: 'My dream acting role is to play a monster or villain'
headlines.description: The Love Island host explains why she is keen to move into acting after joining the cast of The Gentlemen.

headlines.title: 'Tigers and flies': Millions of officials later, why is Xi's corruption purge still going?
headlines.description: Critics say Xi's purges reveal a ruthless drive for absolute loyalty and total control of the military.

headlines.title: New Archbishop of Canterbury to walk 87 mile pilgrimage route ahead of installation
headlines.description: Dame Sarah Mullally's six-day walk begins at Southwark Cathedral and travels along the Becket Way to Canterbury Cathedral.

headlines.title: My stalker tricked random men into turning up at house for sex
headlines.description: A family were visited by strange men, who had seen adverts created by Declan Guess for "fun and sex" online.

headlines.title: Charges dropped against US teens whose teacher died during toilet paper prank
headlines.description: The teacher's family had asked for charges, including one of homicide, to be dropped "to prevent a separate tragedy from occuring" over the prank turned fatal.

headlines.title: F1 races in Middle East to be cancelled because of war in Iran
headlines.description: The Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Grands Prix scheduled for next month are set to be cancelled as a result of the Middle East war.

headlines.title: Austrian glaciers disintegrating due to climate change, say scientists
headlines.description: Scientists say nearly all of the significant glaciers in the Austrian Alps have shrunk.

headlines.title: Pink Floyd guitar sold for record-breaking $14.6m
headlines.description: The guitar was used by David Gilmour on some of Pink Floyd's best-known albums, including Dark Side of the Moon.

headlines.title: I died and came back to life. Now I just want to feel joy every day
headlines.description: Grace Victory says she is more content than ever after spending three months in a coma.

headlines.title: Woman reunited with stolen dog after eight years
headlines.description: Rebecca Harvey says it was an emotional moment when she was reunited with Flora.

headlines.title: Man sails around world in boat he built in his shed
headlines.description: Adam Waugh, from Northumberland, says he feels "on top of the world" after his mammoth journey.

headlines.title: 'Extinct' butterfly found across southern England
headlines.description: There has been a flurry of early sightings of the large tortoiseshell butterfly, a charity says.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Taking Back Control: Why ‘Agency’ Could Be The Next Big Idea In Politics
headlines.description: A top pollster explains how a sense of powerlessness amongst voters is shaping politics.

headlines.title: The Week: Jeremy Bowen On The Iran War
headlines.description: Jeremy Bowen joins Newscast to discuss the latest with the Iran War.

headlines.title: 'Scotland's wild ride from false dawns and fatalism to tilt at history'
headlines.description: Scotland spent decades staring in the window as Ireland and Wales partied in the Six Nations. In Dublin on Saturday, they finally have a shot at glory.

headlines.title: Wood 'improving' but thinking of life after cricket
headlines.description: England bowler Mark Wood says his injured knee is improving but admits he has started thinking about life after cricket for the first time.

headlines.title: Aberg sets Players pace as McIlroy and Scheffler toil
headlines.description: Ludvig Aberg fires a sublime 63 to lead the Players Championship by two shots at halfway as Rory McIlroy and Scottie Scheffler narrowly make the cut.

headlines.title: Brentford, the World Cup and an itch to return to Gaelic football
headlines.description: Brentford and Republic of Ireland captain Nathan Collins tells Kelly Somers about his dreams of the World Cup.

headlines.title: They were told it wasn't for girls, but these could be the future faces of F1
headlines.description: F1 Academy wants to nurture and develop female drivers who can compete at motorsport’s top level

headlines.title: Would Arsenal be 'ugliest title winners in history'? What stats say
headlines.description: While Arsenal continue their challenge on four fronts, their style of play continues to be criticised by some. Is it justified?
